failure_modes.text: Inversion without action leads to paralysis when teams map the failure landscape exhaustively but never commit to the positive moves required to build something.
failure_modes.source_quote: A practitioner who only identifies what to avoid—without subsequently taking positive action—will suffer from paralysis.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pair obstacle removal with positive force augmentation (Lewin): every inverse finding must name both what to avoid and what forward action it unblocks.

failure_modes.text: Straw-man inverse analysis gives false confidence when easily dismissed failure scenarios substitute for genuinely uncomfortable ones that threaten the preferred plan.
failure_modes.source_quote: if inversion is applied without genuine intellectual humility, a person might just generate "straw man" negative scenarios that they easily avoid
failure_modes.mitigation: Use the Dr. Evil Game: imagine you are an evil genius whose sole mission is to sabotage this project — if the team cannot generate uncomfortable answers, the exercise is too shallow.

failure_modes.text: The checklist delusion creates false safety when teams identify specific failure modes, implement safeguards, and falsely believe the system is now foolproof — blinding them to unknown unknowns.
failure_modes.source_quote: A user might identify the five ways a project could fail, implement safeguards for those five things, and falsely believe the system is now foolproof, blinding them to Unknown Unknowns
failure_modes.mitigation: Run controlled experiments to see how the system actually breaks in reality; inverse analysis identifies known failure modes, but unknown unknowns require real-world contact.

failure_modes.text: Fundamental attribution error in failure analysis makes teams attribute hypothetical failure to external uncontrollable circumstances rather than internal controllable decisions.
failure_modes.source_quote: teams might incorrectly attribute hypothetical failure to external, uncontrollable circumstances rather than looking at internal, controllable inputs
failure_modes.mitigation: Apply double-loop learning: question the underlying assumptions and policies that made the failure mode possible, not just the surface action that would trigger it.

premortem_questions.text: If our failure was entirely our own fault — not market conditions, competitors, or bad luck — what internal decision or blind spot destroyed us?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "If our failure was entirely our own fault—and not due to market conditions, competitors, or bad luck—what internal decision or blind spot destroyed us?"

premortem_questions.text: Which of our inverse scenarios, if true, would require us to abandon our current plan entirely — and have we taken that scenario seriously?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "Which of our inverse scenarios, if true, would require us to abandon our current plan entirely — and have we taken that scenario seriously?"

premortem_questions.text: What failure mode are we most confident cannot happen — and what would we do if it happened tomorrow?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What failure mode are we most confident cannot happen — and what would we do if it happened tomorrow?"

heuristics.text: Define the anti-goal first: ask what you don't want to achieve, what causes it, and how to avoid it — the negative formulation often reveals simpler paths than forward planning.
heuristics.source_quote: Rather than setting direct goals, establish "anti-goals." Ask yourself: "What don't I want to achieve? What causes the non-goal? How can I avoid that?"

heuristics.text: Focus on removing obstacles rather than pushing harder on supporting forces — removing friction is frequently more effective and less costly than adding propulsion.
heuristics.source_quote: instead of just pushing harder on the supporting forces, you focus on removing the obstacles (the impeding forces)

heuristics.text: Actively seek to disprove your own hypothesis before defending it — invert confirmation bias by asking where the disconfirming evidence lives.
heuristics.source_quote: rather than looking for evidence to confirm it, use inversion to actively seek ways to disprove your own theory

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on anti-goal formulation, obstacle removal over force augmentation, disprove-it discipline, and the incompleteness warning that inversion without forward action is paralysis.
donor_drops: Did not preserve generic backward-thinking philosophy because the raw source is most useful as a concrete failure-avoidance and hypothesis-disproval method.
open_questions: The Dr. Evil Game heuristic could be its own chunk but is kept as a mitigation because the source uses it in the risk-guarding context, not the primary playbook.

allies: The raw allies section is rich on premortem, second-order-thinking, margin-of-safety, first-principles-thinking, and Occam's razor for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strongest on confirmation-bias, inside-view/planning-fallacy, and commitment-bias pressures.
structured_tensions: The source has explicit structured tension entries for confirmation-bias, commitment-bias, and inside-view — all with clear conflict mechanics.
